mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales directly captures consumer spending momentum, which drives aggregate corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or if the mean 20-day return following positive RSAFS growth is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% triggers market repricing of earnings expectations due to anticipated consumer spending contraction and rising labor cost rigidities, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or if the average 60-day return during these episodes is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive inflation momentum combined with a federal funds rate below 1.0% produces deeply negative real interest rates that drive capital reallocation into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this effect requires both conditions to hold simultaneously.
regime_hint: low nominal rate environment with accelerating inflation
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if a regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns on the interaction term does not yield a positive and significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a rolling 3-year window, or if the interaction adds less than 2 percentage points of R-squared beyond main effects.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals imminent inventory liquidation and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines during late-cycle periods, or if the mean return during these episodes is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts forecast accelerating negative wealth effects and tightening credit conditions for financials and consumer discretionary sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered episodes, or if the average 60-day return following two consecutive HOUST declines is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth relative to the prior quarter indicates strengthening aggregate demand momentum that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth exceeds prior quarter growth
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the rank correlation between GDPC1 growth acceleration and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or if the mean 60-day return following acceleration is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contractions in industrial production and payroll employment signal a broad supply-side and labor-market deterioration that foreshadows negative earnings surprises, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the joint signal of INDPRO and PAYEMS declines does not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term coefficient is not negative and significant (p > 0.05) in a rolling 2-year logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate rising above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, or if the mean 20-day return during these episodes is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).
